title: Upon Skoles. Epig.
content:
Skoles stinks so deadly, that his breeches loath
    His dampish buttocks furthermore to clothe;
    Cloy'd they are up with arse; but hope, one blast
    Will whirl about, and blow them thence at last.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Skoles. Epig.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
